steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the function of the underlined sentence. The underlined sentence states what The Times says: the English ideal is that everyone should be free to do and look as they like. The next sentence begins "But culture indefatigably tries..." — Arnold immediately pushes back, rejecting that ideal.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'It asserts that the English are often subject to unfair critiques of their sense of taste.': The sentence summarizes The Times' defense of the English ideal; it says nothing about whether critiques of the English are fair or unfair. ✗
  • Choice B — 'It presents an opinion with which the author disagrees.': The Times' position — that everyone should do as they like — is exactly what Arnold's "But" rejects. The underlined sentence sets up the opposing view Arnold then argues against. ✓
  • Choice C — 'It introduces an example that supports the author's main assertion.': Arnold's main assertion is that culture draws people toward what is beautiful rather than letting each person do as they please. The Times' position is the opposite of that assertion, not support for it. ✗
  • Choice D — 'It implies that opinions regarding culture evolve over time.': There is no mention of change over time or evolving opinions anywhere in the passage. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
